f_7849697123118957564.text:
Data Wrangling
Experiment – 4
Title of the Experiment: Understanding NumPy Broadcasting and Solving Numerical
Problems Using Arrays
Aim of the Experiment: To understand NumPy broadcasting rules and apply them to solve
numerical problems and simple systems of equations by broadcasting scalars and vectors
across arrays.
Software Required: Python 3.x, Jupyter Notebook, NumPy
Algorithm:
Test Cases:
Tracing of Code:
Expected Output:
1

Data Wrangling
PART A: BROADCASTING BASICS
# Scalar broadcasting
OutPut:
===== PART A: BROADCASTING BASICS =====
Original Data: [10 20 30 40 50]
After Adding Scalar: [15 25 35 45 55]
After Multiplying Scalar: [ 50 100 150 200 250]
# Vector broadcasting
OutPut:
===== VECTOR BROADCASTING =====
Matrix:
[[10 20 30]
[40 50 60]
[70 80 90]]
Row Vector Broadcast:
[[11 22 33]
[41 52 63]
[71 82 93]]
2

Data Wrangling
Column Vector Broadcast:
[[ 20 30 40]
[ 60 70 80]
[100 110 120]]
PART B: SOLVING NUMERICAL PROBLEMS
OutPut:
===== PART B: SOLVING NUMERICAL PROBLEMS =====
x values: [0 1 2 3 4]
y = 2x + 3: [ 3 5 7 9 11]
# Solve multiple equations using vectors
OutPut:
Solutions of multiple equations: [ 5 12 21]
3

Data Wrangling
PART C: EXTENDED BROADCASTING APPLICATIONS
OutPut:
Normalized Scores: [0.45 0.6 0.75 0.9 1. ]
OutPut:
Error Values: [ 2 2 -10 5]
OutPut:
Distances from reference point: [1.41421356 1.41421356 4.24264069]
4

Experiment – 7
Title of the Experiment: Data Type Conversion and Transformation Across CSV, JSON, and
XML Datasets
Aim of the Experiment: To convert data between different types such as string-to-integer,
string-to-float, and string-to-date, and to apply data viewing and cleaning methods on CSV,
JSON, and XML datasets.
